text.title: Docker Security Hardening Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Production containers MUST run as non-root (`USER` set, no `--user 0`); root execution rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Containers MUST `--cap-drop=ALL` and re-add only the minimum needed; default capability set rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Production containers MUST run with `--read-only` root FS + scoped tmpfs writable paths; writable root rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Build secrets MUST use BuildKit `--mount=type=secret` and runtime secrets via Docker Secrets / Vault / IRSA; baked-in creds rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: CI MUST run a vulnerability scan and fail on HIGH+ severity unless an explicit waiver is signed.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Production images MUST be signed (cosign / Notary v2); unsigned images rejected by admission controller.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Skip when sandbox-only image OR isolated cluster with documented risk acceptance.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.
